author: TNN
title: Urgent Action Needed: Retired Officials Demand Punjab CM Address Dariya Pollution | Ludhiana News - Times of India
text: Ludhiana: A group of retired bureaucrats, police officers, and army veterans from the Kirti Kisan Forum has written to Punjab chief minister Bhagwant Mann, demanding quick action to address the pollution in the Buddha Nullah (Buddha Dariya). The forum called for implementing Punjab Pollution Control Board’s orders for shutting the untreated-waste outlets of common effluent treatment plants (CETPs) along Tajpur Road.The PPCB’s directives, is based on what the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) had recommended more than a month ago. They demanded an inquiry into the reasons behind the delay in its implementation and if official negligence had allowed untreated chemical waste to continue polluting the water. The forum suggests that this pollution affects the drinking and irrigation water for more than 2 crore people in Punjab and Rajasthan. They said the toxic contamination of the Buddha Nullah and the Satluj river over four decades has caused cancer and hepatitis among communities living downstream. Buddha Dariya is Satluj’s tributary.Activists from the Kale Pani Da Morcha have engaged with residents near Tajpur Road’s dyeing units, requesting them to join a Dec 3 programme of forcing the authorities concerned into halting pollution. They said the sullied channel remained the only source of drinking water for many, as groundwater had also become unfit. The Kirti Kisan Forum warned against any more delays in executing the PPCB’s orders.We also published the following articles recentlyDelhi pollution: Are Punjab-Haryana farmers hiding fires from satellites?Despite a reported decrease in stubble burning instances in north-west India and Pakistan, experts are raising concerns over potential manipulation of burning timings to avoid satellite detection. Scientists observe discrepancies between reduced fire counts and stagnant aerosol levels, suggesting farmers might be burning crop residue after satellite overpass times.115392826Sprinkler rain as Noida condos try to tackle toxic pollutionTo combat hazardous pollution levels in Noida, housing societies have taken to spraying water from rooftops, hoping to settle dust and pollutants. This initiative, while offering limited local relief, highlights residents' concerns and their call for stronger government action to address the annual smog crisis.115500849What's Delhi smoking: Major pollutants behind toxic NCR smogDelhi-NCR is grappling with 'Severe Plus' air quality, prompting emergency measures. Stubble burning in neighboring states is a major contributor, accounting for a significant portion of Delhi's PM2.5 levels. Vehicular emissions, construction dust, and industrial activities further exacerbate the situation. Restrictions on vehicle use and construction activities are in effect under the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP).115403755
